name: Muslim Association of Puget Sound
mission: PNW Seminary provides a structured program for adults to deepen their understanding of Islamic scholarship and leadership.
theoryOfChange: Investing in education (scholarships/seminaries) and social services (clinics/assistance) to produce leaders and a stable community that follows the prophetic example of service.
scoreSummary: Muslim Association of Puget Sound shows strong impact and good alignment, with zakat compliance.

evaluationSources: Support for Pathway to Tech Project (Senator Patty Murray)

zakatClaimEvidence: Zakat (Almsgiving). Fulfill your religious obligation of almsgiving (Zakat) to purify your wealth. Zakat funds will be distributed by MAPS to those in need, in adherence to Islamic guidelines. Give Your Zakat Now! (Source: https://secure.myvanco.com/YHBR/home)

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Good donor alignment with manageable risk. Evidence quality is solid but still evolving in places.

zakat.claim_evidence: Zakat (Almsgiving). Fulfill your religious obligation of almsgiving (Zakat) to purify your wealth. Zakat funds will be distributed by MAPS to those in need, in adherence to Islamic guidelines. Give Your Zakat Now! (Source: https://secure.myvanco.com/YHBR/home)

all_citations.claim: The organization reported total revenue of $7,286,362 and a program expense ratio of 81.7%.
The PNW Seminary provides structured programs for adult Islamic scholarship and leadership.
The organization tracks total enrollments and congregational attendance as key metrics.
MAPS provides direct services including food/gas cards, medical clinic access, and permanent shelter for women.
The charity offers multiple donation options and giving methods for supporters.
The organization engages the community through volunteer opportunities such as senior transportation.
The organization is ZAKAT-ELIGIBLE and maintains policies for distributing funds to valid asnaf categories.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: MAPS achieves a solid GMG score of 71/100 due to its high program efficiency and strong alignment with donor needs, though its overall score is tempered by medium data confidence. The organization demonstrates significant financial health and a direct-service impact model.
areas_for_improvement: Increasing data transparency to improve the medium data confidence level.
Expanding third-party impact verification to further validate cost-per-beneficiary metrics.

dimension_explanations.alignment: MAPS shows high donor fit and addresses urgent religious and cultural needs through its ZAKAT-ELIGIBLE status and specialized programs like the PNW Seminary [2][10]. The organization maintains a strong track record of community engagement through volunteer opportunities and structured giving [7][8].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization demonstrates a direct-service impact model with a total revenue of $7,286,362 and a high program expense ratio of 81.7% [1]. Its effectiveness is evidenced by diverse outcomes ranging from medical clinic services to housing support for women and educational enrollments [5][6].

baseline_narrative.headline: The Muslim Association of Puget Sound serves as a vital hub for Islamic scholarship, leadership development, and comprehensive community support in the Pacific Northwest.
strengths: High program expense ratio of 81.7%, indicating a strong commitment to direct service delivery.
Exceptional fundraising efficiency, spending only $0.02 to raise every $1.
Robust financial stability with 17.3 months of working capital available.
baseline_narrative.summary: The Muslim Association of Puget Sound (MAPS) provides a structured environment for daily prayers and educational programs, including the PNW Seminary which focuses on deepening Islamic scholarship and leadership [1][2]. The organization is recognized as ZAKAT-ELIGIBLE, allowing it to mobilize religious giving toward essential community services such as food assistance and housing support [6][10].

alignment.explanation: The organization has <cite id="9">high Muslim donor fit</cite> as a zakat-eligible entity serving the <cite id="10">fuqara and masakin</cite> categories. It serves as a vital <cite id="2">religious and cultural hub</cite> for the Pacific Northwest Muslim community.
alignment.improvement: Alignment scores could increase by <cite id="21">expanding services to more underserved geographies</cite> with limited existing nonprofit coverage.

credibility.explanation: Donors can trust MAPS due to its <cite id="1">20-year track record</cite> and <cite id="15">100% independent board of directors</cite>. The organization maintains <cite id="8">17.3 months of operating reserves</cite>, indicating high financial reliability.
credibility.improvement: Credibility could be further enhanced by increasing data transparency to move beyond the current <cite id="18">medium data confidence level</cite>.

impact.explanation: MAPS delivers direct services with a <cite id="19">cost per beneficiary of $778.64</cite>, which is considered strong for the global education and religious sector. They successfully mobilized <cite id="20">$450,000 for small businesses</cite> and provided housing stability for dozens of families.
impact.improvement: Impact measurement would benefit from <cite id="16">developing a more explicit theory of change</cite> to better communicate long-term outcomes.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who want to support a comprehensive Islamic community hub that combines religious education with direct social services in the US.
donor_motivations: Want to fulfill zakat through a local US-based institution
Value high financial efficiency and low fundraising overhead
Care about supporting Islamic scholarship and leadership development
Prefer organizations with a long-standing physical presence in the community
giving_considerations: Donations support a wide range of programs from prayers to small business grants
The organization is in a growth phase with a 20.8% revenue CAGR
Focus is primarily domestic within the United States
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking international humanitarian relief or those who require high-tier transparency seals like Candid Platinum.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade C reflects verified annual outcomes and beneficiary tracking, though rigorous external evaluations are not yet present.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: MAPS creates impact by providing spiritual infrastructure, academic religious training, and emergency financial assistance to stabilize and empower the local Muslim community.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Tracking metrics like the <cite id="11">$75,000 in distributed aid</cite> ensures that donor funds are reaching those in immediate need.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: With a <cite id="23">20.8% revenue growth rate</cite>, MAPS is expanding its seminary and social service capacity to meet rising community demand.

strengths.detail: The organization maintains a <cite id="3">program expense ratio of 81.7%</cite> and an industry-leading fundraising efficiency of <cite id="4">$0.02 per $1 raised</cite>.
MAPS holds <cite id="8">17.3 months of working capital</cite>, providing a significant buffer for long-term operational sustainability.
The organization is <cite id="9">zakat-eligible</cite> and directs funds toward specific asnaf including <cite id="10">the poor (fuqara) and needy (masakin)</cite>.

strengths_deep_dive: MAPS distinguishes itself through a multi-faceted impact model that combines spiritual growth with tangible social safety nets. In 2024, the organization distributed <cite id="11">$75,000 in food and gas cards</cite> and reached <cite id="12">20,000 small businesses</cite> through its outreach programs. Their educational reach is equally robust, with <cite id="13">3,000 total enrollments</cite> across various programs and <cite id="14">950 students</cite> tracked in more recent academic sessions. This breadth of service is supported by a <cite id="15">100% independent board</cite>, ensuring high standards of governance and oversight.
rich_narrative.summary: Founded in <cite id="1">2006</cite>, the Muslim Association of Puget Sound (MAPS) has evolved into a cornerstone of the Pacific Northwest's Islamic infrastructure. The organization operates a diverse portfolio of services ranging from <cite id="2">daily prayers and educational programs</cite> to critical social services. MAPS demonstrates a high level of operational efficiency, maintaining a <cite id="3">program expense ratio of 81.7%</cite>, which ensures that the vast majority of donor funds directly support community initiatives. Their financial model is exceptionally lean, spending only <cite id="4">$0.01 to raise every $1</cite> in contributions. Beyond religious services, the organization has achieved significant scale in humanitarian outreach, serving <cite id="5">20,500 Iftar meals</cite> and supporting <cite id="6">70 families to prevent eviction</cite> in recent cycles. The organization's <cite id="7">PNW Seminary</cite> provides a structured academic pathway for adults to deepen their understanding of Islamic scholarship, further cementing MAPS's role as a center for leadership development.
